exemplar.title: M25 #4: Do the ever-improving tools of an area of knowledge always result in improved knowledge? Discuss with reference to two areas of knowledge.

detailedExemplarData.title: M25 #4: Do the ever-improving tools of an area of knowledge always result in improved knowledge? Discuss with reference to two areas of knowledge.

strengths: Maintains a clear focus on the prescribed title, with definitions of key terms (âever-improving tools,â âimproved knowledgeâ).
Effectively structures the discussion around two areas of knowledge (natural sciences and the arts).
Integrates four relevant, discipline-appropriate examples to support its claims (evolution of the periodic table; LHC false positive; re-attribution of a painting; AI-generated art).
Demonstrates awareness of alternative perspectives by contrasting benefits and limitations of technological advances and acknowledging ethical objections to AI art.
weaknesses: Predominantly descriptive recounting of examples without sustained analysis of how knowledge itself is transformed.
Links to underlying TOK concepts (e.g. nature of evidence, paradigms, interpretation, ethics) are noted but not deeply explored.
Cross-area-of-knowledge synthesis is limited, and evaluation of differing viewpoints is brief or asserted rather than critically examined.
Logical progression is uneven, with occasional repetition and under-developed transitions between claims and counterclaims.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The official examiner awarded an overall mark of 5, placing the essay solidly in the 5â6 band. The submission stays on task, links to two areas of knowledge, provides relevant examples, and shows some awareness of different viewpoints, thereby fulfilling the core requirements of the band. However, the work remains largely descriptive, lacks depth in evaluating the implications of its arguments, and offers limited critical synthesis across areas of knowledge. On balance, a score of 5 (mid-band for 5â6) accurately reflects the strengths and limitations identified in the strand assessment.

annotations.comment: The ethical and monetary consequences are mentioned but not fully evaluated; the student could analyze the implications of re-attribution for cultural authority and value systems.
The discussion of AI-generated art is timely and shows awareness of both technological capabilities and ethical concerns about authorship.
Claims that AI lacks subjective experience would benefit from linking to theory on emotion and imagination as Ways of Knowing to substantiate why human artists contribute unique knowledge.
The conclusion effectively summarizes both AoK discussions and reiterates the interactive relationship between tools and knowledge.
The conclusion is general; to reach a higher level, the student should consider wider implications for knowledge communities or suggest areas for further inquiry.
The final statement calls for critical perspective but could be strengthened by proposing specific knowledge questions or methodologies for evaluating tool limitations.
References are comprehensive but inconsistent in formatting style; adopting a clear citation convention would enhance academic rigor.
The attribution shift of âThe Man with the Golden Helmetâ is an apt case that shows how art-historical knowledge relies on evolving technical analyses.
The inclusion of the image is effective, but the student should discuss how imaging tools challenge interpretive frameworks and the nature of evidence in art history.

The student clearly defines âever-improving toolsâ and âimproved knowledge,â establishing a focused framework for the essay and demonstrating awareness of the prescribed title and relevant Areas of Knowledge.

The definitions remain broad and descriptive; to deepen analysis, the student should explicitly link these concepts to a central knowledge question, exploring how tool improvement alters our understanding of evidence.
Restating the prescribed title at the outset reinforces relevance and ensures the discussion remains aligned with the essay question.
To reach a higher level, the student should explicitly introduce a knowledge question that encapsulates the tension between tool advancement and knowledge reliability.
The introduction of two Areas of Knowledge (natural sciences and the arts) structures the essay effectively and shows awareness of alternative perspectives.

The thesis asserts that interpretation matters but lacks indication of the specific implications to be examined; outlining the criteria for evaluating âimproved knowledgeâ would clarify the analysis.
The periodic table example is well chosen and demonstrates how technological advances can transform a classification system into a predictive scientific model.
This discussion remains largely descriptive; to advance, the student should analyze how changes in measurement precision affect the underlying-assumptions of scientific paradigms.
The Isaac Newton quote effectively illustrates the cumulative nature of scientific knowledge and reinforces the argument about generational tool improvement.
The quote is used as reinforcement rather than for critical analysis; the student could discuss the limitations of the âstanding on shouldersâ metaphor in interpreting scientific progress.
The recognition of bias in overconfidence toward advanced tools shows awareness of counterclaims and the need for critical thinking in the natural sciences.
This paragraph identifies bias but does not link it to ways of knowing; the student should examine how trust and perception as WOKs shape scientistsâ interpretation of data.
The LHC false-positive example is a strong RLS that vividly illustrates how even sophisticated instruments can mislead without critical validation.

While two perspectives are presented, the essay does not evaluate their broader implications for knowledge-production in physics; a deeper examination of how consensus emerges would strengthen analysis.
The transition to the Arts section is abrupt; adding a linking sentence would improve flow and coherence between Areas of Knowledge.
A clearer topic sentence forecasting the Arts discussion would improve coherence and signal the shift between Areas of Knowledge.
The phrase âever-improving toolsâ is repeated extensively; varying terminology and integrating TOK vocabulary would improve stylistic maturity.

described by levels
1â5 or is not a
response to one of
the prescribed titles
on the list for the
current session.
The discussion is weakly connected to the title. While there may be links to the areas of knowledge, any relevant points are descriptive or consist only of unsupported assertions.

The discussion is connected to the title and makes superficial or limited links to areas of knowledge. The discussion is largely descriptive. Limited arguments are offered but they are unclear and are not supported by effective examples.

The discussion
is focused on
the title and is
developed with
some links to
areas of
knowledge.
Arguments are
offered and are
supported by
examples.
There is some
awareness of
different points
of view.

The discussion is focused on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and supported by examples. There is awareness and some evaluation of different points of view.

The discussion has a sustained focus on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and effectively supported by specific examples. The implications of arguments are considered. There is clear awareness and evaluation of different points of view.
